context: Sunday pre-week read. Crypto is consolidating a sharp macro-driven recovery: BTC +8.7% off Wednesday's 21-month low ($57.8K) after June NFP printed +57K vs 110K expected (Thu Jul 2, released early for the holiday) with −74K of back-revisions — a dovish shift into the end-of-July FOMC — reinforced by Warsh comments easing inflation fears. Flows turned with the tape: Thursday's +$221.7M spot-ETF inflow was the largest in over two months and snapped a 10-day, −$2.7B outflow streak that made June the worst ETF month on record (−$4.06B). Sentiment is still Extreme Fear (F&G 23, but rising three days straight), which keeps this a half-size, pullback-entry regime. XRP is the idiosyncratic story: +8.9% on the week INTO a delayed CLARITY binary — Senate returns Jul 13, floor vote now late Jul/Aug, prediction markets at ~42%. Plan: buy mapped OTE zones on sweeps, trim at TP1, nothing chased above range highs until Monday's full-liquidity session validates the bounce.

tickers.thesis: Best 7d performer of the four (+8.9%) into a DELAYED binary: the Senate left for the holiday without a CLARITY floor vote, killing the White House Jul-4 target — chamber returns Jul 13, vote window now late Jul/Aug before campaign season closes the calendar. Prediction markets de-rated passage to ~42% (from ~73% peak) while price rallied — odds and tape are diverging; one is wrong. Structure: three-day impulse $1.045→$1.184, now consolidating above the $1.083 breakout shelf. Constructive while $1.083 holds; this is a pullback-buy tape, not a chase, until the whip-count news catches up.
levels: June supply shelf + round number; above = Jun 15 spike $1.29
Jul 4 impulse high; BSL resting above
Jul 1 low; below = Jun 26 flush low $1.009

setup.entry: OTE long $1.074–1.098 (62–79% of $1.045→$1.184 leg; $1.083 shelf inside zone)
setup.stop: $1.028 — below Jul 2 low $1.045 with ~1.6% buffer, beyond the visible pool
setup.invalidation: Daily close < $1.05, or CLARITY odds < 30% on fresh whip-count news

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 23 (Extreme Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 23 (Extreme Fear) at 07:47Z — +1 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Bad news is good news again — the bounce is a rates trade
insights.body: The +8.7% recovery started the minute NFP missed (+57K vs 110K, unemployment 4.2%, Apr+May revised −74K): markets repriced the end-of-July FOMC dovish and crypto followed rates, not crypto-native news. That means the bounce lives or dies on macro — respect it while BTC holds $61.25K daily, but a hot CPI print in the Jul 13 week reverses the whole logic. One caveat inside the report: average hourly earnings ticked UP to 3.5% — the wage tail risk is not zero.

insights.title: The whale/ETF divergence is resolving — watch for the second green day
insights.body: Two weeks of on-chain accumulation (~270K BTC, ~$16.7B, concentrated near $59K) ran directly against a record −$4.06B June ETF bleed. Thursday's +$221.7M inflow — largest in >2 months, ending a 10-day streak — is the first sign the institutional seller is done. One day is noise; a second consecutive green flow print Monday makes it a trend and removes the supply that capped every June bounce.

insights.title: XRP: delayed binary ≠ dead binary — trade the odds, not the vote
insights.body: The Senate left without voting, the Jul-4 target is dead, and prediction markets de-rated CLARITY to ~42% from 73%. But the bill already cleared committee bipartisan, sits on the Senate calendar, and the floor window (late Jul/Aug, before campaign season) is intact. Price +8.9% against falling odds means someone is front-running a whip-count improvement — the tradeable event is any Dem-crossover headline after Jul 13, not the vote itself. Standard Chartered's $4–8B ETF-inflow projection is the pass-scenario anchor.

insights.title: Extreme Fear playbook: sweeps only, half size
insights.body: F&G at 23 (up from 21 three days ago) with price printing higher lows is the classic early regime-turn shape — but 'early' is the operative word. In this regime the edge is sweep-and-reclaim entries at mapped OTE zones with stops beyond the pool, not breakout chases: every breakout in June died into the lower-high shelf ($65.6K BTC). Half size until F&G reclaims 30+ or BTC reclaims $65.6K.

insights.title: Monday is the real test — holiday tape proves nothing
insights.body: The entire bounce printed across a US holiday stretch (NFP moved to Thursday, markets shut Friday, weekend since): thin books flatter rallies. Monday Jul 6 brings the first full-liquidity US session plus a fresh ETF flow print. Hold-above $61.25K on real volume = add-on signal; a Monday fade back below it = the bounce was a short-cover mirage and the $59.5K/$57.8K pools come back into play.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 23 (Extreme Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "Mapping with In-Memory Layers to Reduce LLM Overload" (↑15)

Whale wallets absorbed ~270K BTC (~$16.7B) in two weeks, concentrated near $59K, while June ETFs bled a record −$4.06B — a rare measurable split between on-chain accumulation and institutional distribution. The side that blinks first sets Q3 direction; ETF flows just blinked (+$221.7M Thu).

Thursday's +$221.7M spot-BTC ETF inflow was the largest single-day intake in over two months and ended a 10-day, −$2.7B outflow streak. June's −$4.06B surpassed the old monthly record (−$3.56B, Feb 2025) and pushed cumulative 2026 flows negative — the flip, if it holds, is happening from maximum bearish positioning.

NFP internals were worse than the +57K headline: leisure/hospitality −61K and Apr+May revised down a combined −74K. Labor is cooling faster than the headline shows — dovish for the end-of-July FOMC — but average hourly earnings ticked UP to 3.5%, so the CPI print in the Jul 13 week is the tape's real judge.

Prediction markets price CLARITY passage at ~42%, down from ~73% earlier this year — while XRP rallied +8.9% on the week. Odds and price are diverging; either the tape is front-running a whip-count improvement or the rally is short-covering. Watch the odds, not the price, after the Senate returns Jul 13.

Fear & Greed ground up 21→22→23 over three days while price printed higher lows — the same sub-25 divergence shape that has historically marked regime floors. Still Extreme Fear: the signal says start scaling, not size up.

Canary's spot HBAR ETF logged another ZERO-flow week even as HBAR led majors at +3.9% — the only ticker on this list rallying with a confirmed institutional no-show. Thin-volume ($0.09B) strength without a flow bid is fade-prone; demand volume confirmation before trusting HBAR breakouts.
